causal_question: How did municipal adoption of a civil service commission between 1910 and 1920 change the share of blue-collar city jobs held by foreign-born white residents in subsequent decennial censuses, once the city's foreign-born white population share is taken into account?
vague_question: Did a city's decision to set up a civil service commission lead to more or fewer foreign-born white people holding blue-collar city jobs later on?
